curatedFacts.founded: 2001 (amalgamation), mining since 1883, created by merger of regional municipality
curatedFacts.landmarks: Science North, Dynamic Earth, Big Nickel (9m tall), Bell Park, Lake Ramsey, Sudbury Basin
curatedFacts.notable: known as 'The Nickel Capital' — largest city in Northern Ontario, dramatically transformed from barren mining landscape to green city through world-renowned regreening program
curatedFacts.economy: mining capital of Canada (nickel, copper) — Vale, Glencore, Laurentian University, Northern Ontario School of Medicine, mining technology
curatedFacts.entityDesc: Greater Sudbury is a city in Northern Ontario, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 166,004. Mining capital of canada (nickel, copper) — vale, glencore, laurentian university, northern ontario school of medicine, mining technology.
curatedFacts.longDesc:
Greater Sudbury isn't just Ontario's city — it's a place where ambition meets opportunity. Founded mining since 1883, created by merger of regional municipality, this city has transformed into a thriving community of over ~166,000 people.

What makes Greater Sudbury remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Character and community. The city encompasses everything from over 330 lakes within city limits, extensive trail system, vibrant Franco-Ontarian culture, Northern Film & Music Festival. Each neighborhood has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by mining capital of Canada (nickel, copper) — Vale, Glencore, Laurentian University, Northern Ontario School of Medicine, mining technology. This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.

Community features include Science North, Dynamic Earth, Big Nickel (9m tall), Bell Park, Lake Ramsey, Sudbury Basin, which give Greater Sudbury its distinctive character.
